steps.title: Factor the quadratic
steps.content: Look for two numbers that multiply to $-24$ and add to $10$. Those numbers are $12$ and $-2$, so $$z^2+10z-24=(z+12)(z-2).$$

steps.title: Use the zero product property
steps.content: Set each factor equal to zero: $$z+12=0 \quad \text{or} \quad z-2=0.$$ This gives $z=-12$ or $z=2$.

steps.title: Enter one valid solution
steps.content: The problem asks for one solution, so either root is acceptable. Entering 2 is valid. In Desmos, the $x$-intercepts of the graph are $-12$ and $2$.
